title: Final, Agreed-Upon Text

mood.label: Held breath
mood.summary: A near-final but unsigned U.S.–Iran deal and steady markets lift the day to the upper edge of the mixed band, while a deadly Mindanao quake aftermath, famine in Darfur and Ebola in eastern Congo keep the relief provisional — the World Cup's joyful return and a run of quiet science wins say the breath is hopeful, but it's still a breath held, not let go.

style.genre: motorik / krautrock, Rhodes-ish electric piano over a TR-909 pulse

sampleSources: Strudel default library — sampled piano (filtered into a Rhodes-ish electric piano), vibraphone (VCSL) for the topline, RolandTR909 drum machine for the motorik pulse; supporting sine sub-bass and a low sawtooth pad
pattern: stack(s("bd*4, ~ ~ hh ~ ~ ~ hh ~ ~ ~ hh ~ ~ ~ hh ~, ~ ~ ~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ rim ~").bank("RolandTR909").gain(0.42).hpf(50).room(0.16).rsize(4), note("d1 ~ ~ ~ d1 ~ a1 ~ ~ ~ bf1 ~ ~ ~ c2 ~").s("sine").attack(0.01).release(0.42).lpf(420).gain(0.5), note("<[d3,f3,a3,c4] [g2,bf2,d3,e3] [bf2,d3,f3] [a2,cs3,e3,g3]>").s("piano").lpf(sine.range(650,1650).slow(16)).gain(0.3).attack(0.01).release(0.5).room(0.34).rsize(5).delay(0.16).delaytime(0.288).pan(0.42).slow(2), note("<[d4,a4,c5] [d4,bf4,e5] [d4,bf4,f5] [cs4,a4,e5]>").s("sawtooth").attack(3.5).decay(1.5).sustain(0.4).release(5).lpf(sine.range(480,1100).slow(28)).gain(0.06).room(0.6).rsize(8), note("a4 ~ ~ c5 ~ ~ b4 ~ ~ ~ a4 ~ ~ cs5 ~ d5 ~ ~ ~ ~ a4 ~ g4 ~ ~ ~ f4 ~ ~ e4 ~ ~").s("vibraphone").gain(0.34).room(0.44).rsize(6).pan(0.58).delay(0.2).delaytime(0.346).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.11).pan(0.7).lpf(2000)).gain(sine.range(0.28,0.4).slow(18)))

lines: Pakistan says it's a final, agreed-upon text, fourteen points and a name on the line,
Vance flies to Europe to sign by the weekend, the strait swings open in thirty days' time —
but the ministry says nothing's been settled at all, and the drones still fall over the water tonight,
so I read the same hope two opposite ways, and I keep the engine running, keep the wheels rolling right.
Out in Mindanao they're counting past sixty, three hundred thousand still under the dust,
El Fasher goes hungry, the Congo runs fevered, the ground keeps reminding me what I can't trust —
but the Cup came home to a four-to-one roar, Canada's first point earned in the light,
and a marine reserve the size of France, and a drop of blood that sees cancer years out of sight.
It's a held breath, not a sigh of relief, a deal in the air that nobody's signed,
the same beat driving, the road going forward, the dark and the bright running side by side —
I'll take the maybe, the almost, the text we agreed on, and carry the ledger that doesn't yet add,
the quake and the famine, the Cup and the cure, the held breath hopeful, the road still ahead.
